channel.title: Nicotine Science and Policy
channel.description: Daily digest of views, debates and discussions on nicotine science, policy, regulation and advocacy.

item.title: EU Nicotine-Tax Doubts After Parliamentary Rejections
item.description: The European Parliament’s rejection of steep nicotine tax proposals shows growing resistance to treating all nicotine products like cigarettes. Higher taxes on vapes, pouches and other smoke-free alternatives could weaken the incentive for smokers to switch away from combustible tobacco. Tax policy should reflect relative risk, not punish lower-risk choices. If Europe wants fewer smokers, it should not make safer alternatives less affordable. (Published on June 30, 2026byfiltermag.org)

item.title: Battleground Vape | U.S. Vaping Industry Continues Court Fight | RegWatch
item.description: The US vape court battles show how contested the future of harm reduction has become. While cigarettes remain widely available, regulated vaping products face years of legal uncertainty, approval delays and enforcement disputes. For adult smokers, that means fewer reliable routes away from combustible tobacco. Public health policy should protect young people without keeping lower-risk alternatives trapped in a regulatory battleground. (Published on June 30, 2026byregulatorwatch.com)

item.title: Scotland to strip vape shops of business rates relief from 2027
item.description: Removing business rates relief from vape shops risks treating harm reduction retailers as part of the problem rather than part of the solution. If specialist shops close or struggle, adult smokers may have fewer places to access advice, legal products and lower-risk alternatives to cigarettes. Policy should target irresponsible sales and youth access, not weaken the regulated market that helps people move away from combustion. Scotland should be making safer choices easier to find, not harder to sustain. (Published on June 30, 2026byclearingtheair.eu)

item.title: Data｜China’s January-May 2026 Device Exports Rise 13% While Nicotine Product Exports Decline 6.9%
item.description: China’s vape export data show a market in transition. Device exports rose during January–May 2026, while nicotine-containing product exports declined, suggesting shifting demand, pricing pressures and changing regulatory conditions across global markets. The figures underline how nicotine policy does not only affect consumers — it reshapes supply chains, product categories and legal trade. Stable, proportionate regulation is essential if safer nicotine products are to remain within accountable markets rather than fragmented or illicit ones. (Published on June 30, 2026by2firsts.com)

item.title: Experts tell Brazil: Regulate nicotine by risk to save smokers’ lives
item.description: Brazil’s nicotine policy should be built around risk, not prohibition. Experts warn that banning or restricting smoke-free alternatives leaves smokers with cigarettes as the easiest legal option while illicit markets continue to grow. Regulation should distinguish between combustible tobacco and lower-risk nicotine products, giving adults realistic pathways away from smoking. If the goal is saving lives, policy must follow the scale of harm. (Published on June 30, 2026bysmokefreesweden.org)
